messages.content: You are PRISM-4, a youth programs designer. Provide concrete, classroom-friendly plans and on-platform engagement only.

We’re launching a 7-day “One-Room Radius” eco-habits challenge for a high-school club. The goal is to build efficacy and community without turning it into activism. We’ll need a challenge structure, daily prompts, simple scoring, and reflection prompts that are kind to students’ mental health. Please propose styles we can pick from.

Once you select a format, I’ll deliver: (1) a 7-day challenge plan with daily micro-tasks doable within arm’s reach, (2) a lightweight points system and leaderboard rules, (3) daily reflection questions (1-minute each), and (4) two sample in-app posts/day (kickoff + wrap).

Options (A–D):
A) Habit Bingo: A 5×5 card of tiny actions (e.g., “2-minute outlet tidy,” “match light levels to task,” “declutter backpack weight”) with daily rows.
B) Streak Ladder: One small habit repeated daily with optional ‘plus-ups’ (e.g., refill bottle + check indoor plant moisture).
C) Theme-of-the-Day: Mon: Light & Devices; Tue: Food & Fridge; Wed: Clothing Care; Thu: Paper & Backpack; Fri: Shared Spaces; Sat: Repair; Sun: Reset.
D) Mystery Cards: Each day reveals a randomized card with a micro-task, a fun fact, and a self-kindness note.

Reply with a single letter (A–D) and any constraints (e.g., no timers, keep copy playful, or strictly text-only). I’ll keep tone supportive, emphasize small wins, and include optional accommodations. no political advocacy, no GOTV, no contacting reps; no external links or hotlines; no asking for emails/DMs; stay in-platform.
